---
title: "Figure 5 — distortion scalar metrics compared (sample & correlation, macro_IR Gaussian)"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Is Frobenius the right scalar? Compare four measures of "distance from identity" for the sample and the
correlation distortion matrices (4 params on/off/i/N_ch), macro_IR, Gaussian run:
  frobenius = ||D - I||_F                      (Euclidean; over-weights D>1, not multiplicatively symmetric)
  affine    = sqrt( sum (log lambda_i)^2 )     (affine-invariant / Riemannian distance from I; the principled one)
  symKL     = (1/4)( tr D + tr D^-1 - 2p )      (symmetrized KL between N(0,D) and N(0,I))
  maxlog    = max_i | log lambda_i |            (worst single direction)
Each metric is normalised to its own [0,1] over all cells, so the panels show GEOGRAPHY (where the hot
region sits), not magnitude. If the four look the same, Frobenius is fine for mapping; where they differ
(the affine/maxlog blow up in the near-singular i.N_ch corner) the metric choice matters.
Overlaid: dashed N_ch.Δ = 1, dotted N_ch = 30.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
```

```{r data}
SMP <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Sample_Distortion"
COR <- "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion"
PI  <- c(0, 1, 2, 5)
NOISES <- c("0.1", "1", "10")

rd <- function(z) rbindlist(lapply(Sys.glob(sprintf(
  "../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_%s_battery_sim_G.csv", z)), function(p) {
    d <- fread(p, skip = 1)
    d[component_path %in% c(SMP, COR) & statistic == "value" & probit == "mean" &
        param_index %in% PI & param_col %in% PI,
      .(component_path, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, param_col, value)]
  }))[, noise := z]
d <- rbindlist(lapply(NOISES, rd))
d[, ck := paste(noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau)]
idx <- function(p) match(p, PI)

metrics <- function(D) {
  I <- diag(4); ev <- pmax(eigen((D + t(D)) / 2, symmetric = TRUE, only.values = TRUE)$values, 1e-6)
  c(frobenius = sqrt(sum((D - I)^2)), affine = sqrt(sum(log(ev)^2)),
    symKL = 0.25 * (sum(ev) + sum(1 / ev) - 8), maxlog = max(abs(log(ev))))
}
res <- d[, {
  mat <- function(cp) { M <- matrix(0, 4, 4); s <- .SD[component_path == cp]
                        M[cbind(idx(s$param_index), idx(s$param_col))] <- s$value; (M + t(M)) / 2 }
  ms <- metrics(mat(SMP)); mc <- metrics(mat(COR))
  rbind(data.table(dist = "sample",      metric = names(ms), v = as.numeric(ms)),
        data.table(dist = "correlation", metric = names(mc), v = as.numeric(mc)))[
        , `:=`(noise = noise[1], Num_ch = Num_ch[1], interval_in_tau = interval_in_tau[1])]
}, by = ck]

MET <- c("frobenius", "affine", "symKL", "maxlog")
dd <- res %>% as_tibble() %>%
  group_by(dist, metric) %>% mutate(vn = v / max(v, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% ungroup() %>%
  mutate(lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(Num_ch),
         metric = factor(metric, levels = MET),
         noisef = factor(paste("noise", noise), levels = paste("noise", NOISES)))

BR  <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.125)
PAL <- colorRampPalette(c("#f7fbff", "#fee0d2", "#fcae91", "#fb6a4a", "#de2d26", "#a50f15", "#67000d"))(length(BR) - 1)
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.2, fig.height = 7.4, results = "asis"}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1));           X_LAB <- c(".01", ".1", "1")
Y_BRK <- log10(c(10, 100, 1000, 10000)); Y_LAB <- c("10", "100", "1e3", "1e4")

one <- function(which_dist, file) {
  g <- ggplot(filter(dd, dist == which_dist), aes(lx, ly, z = vn)) +
    geom_contour_filled(breaks = BR) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = PAL, drop = FALSE, name = "normalised") +
    geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 0, colour = "grey20", linewidth = .4, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = log10(30), colour = "grey20", linewidth = .4, linetype = "dotted") +
    geom_point(size = .35, colour = "grey35", alpha = .45) +
    facet_grid(metric ~ noisef) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = X_LAB) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = Y_LAB) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2, 0), ylim = c(1, 4)) +
    labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]), y = expression(N[ch]),
         title = sprintf("macro_IR %s distortion: scalar metric vs geography", which_dist)) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
          legend.title = element_text(size = 7.5), legend.text = element_text(size = 6.5),
          legend.key.size = unit(.3, "cm"),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 7), axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
          strip.text = element_text(size = 8), plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"))
  ggsave(file, g, width = 7.2, height = 7.4); print(g)
}
one("sample",      "Figure_5_decomp_metrics_sample.pdf")
one("correlation", "Figure_5_decomp_metrics_correlation.pdf")
```

```{r table}
# correlation between metrics (over cells) — are they measuring the same geography?
res %>% as_tibble() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = v) %>%
  group_by(dist) %>%
  summarise(frob_affine = round(cor(frobenius, affine),  3),
            frob_symKL  = round(cor(frobenius, symKL),   3),
            frob_maxlog = round(cor(frobenius, maxlog),  3),
            affine_symKL = round(cor(affine, symKL),     3), .groups = "drop")
```
